standard: Sóc Trăng tao gọi là cốm ống nhé chúng mày
A: In Soc Trang, I call it "com ong" (tube-shaped rice flakes), guys.
B: In Soc Trang, I call it cốm ống, you guys.

A: You should focus on making the ladies look beautiful. Now that I know the address of the beauty salon, let's go get pampered sometime!
B: You should focus on getting beauty treatments for the ladies; you already know the address, so let's drop by and get some beauty treatments sometime.
C: Focus on beautifying for the sisters, I already know a beauty salon address, let's drop by sometime and get beautified together.

A: Regarding frying food for this meal.
B: Fry it up for them to eat.
C: About this Chiên treating us to food.

standard: Đầu bạc thì làm sao?

B: What if my hair turns gray?
C: So what if I have white hair?

standard: Thích cái nào cứ chọn
A: Choose whichever one you like.
B: Just pick whichever one you like.
C: Pick whichever one you like

standard: Học cái thói này ở đâu về đây
A: Where did you learn this habit?
B: Where did you learn this habit?
C: Where did you pick up this habit and bring it here?

standard: Ngày nay ít người đi bắt tắc kè rồi đấy.
A: These days, very few people go gecko hunting.
B: Nowadays, few people go catching geckos anymore.
C: These days, few people go catch geckos anymore.

A: That's a nice planned house over there, isn't it?
B: The urban planning on the other side of the house looks nice, doesn't it?
C: The planned houses over there look nice, huh?

standard: Ai bảo lủi vào đấy làm gì, ỉa trong góc đi là sẽ giải tán đám đông.
A: Who told you to sneak in there? Just defecate in the corner and the crowd will disperse.
B: Who told you to sneak in there? Just take a dump in the corner and the crowd will disperse.
C: Who told you to sneak in there for? Just go take a dump in the corner and the crowd will disperse.

standard: Lâu nay em lo làm ăn quá nên quên luôn chuyện mạng với Zalo, Facebook này kia đi rồi.
A: I've been so busy with work lately that I completely forgot about the internet, Zalo, Facebook, and all that stuff.
B: I've been so focused on my business for a while now that I completely forgot about social media, Zalo, Facebook, and all that.
C: For a long time, I've been so busy working that I completely forgot about social media, Zalo, Facebook and all that.

standard: Ai lại đi đồn. Có thật mà đồn làm gì mất công vậy?
A: Why would anyone spread rumors? If it's true, why bother spreading them?
B: Who is spreading rumors? It's true, so why go through the trouble of spreading rumors?
C: Who would go around spreading rumors like that? If it's true, why bother talking about it? What a waste of effort.

A: They won't let us play football at Tru Market, will they?
B: They won't let us play football at the Tru market pitch, right?
C: I'm not allowed to play at Chợ Trụ field, I wonder why?

standard: Tớ người Tiền Hải chuyên dùng từ rót nước.
A: I'm from Tien Hai and I often use the phrase "pouring water."
B: I am from Tien Hai and I specialize in using the term "rót nước."
C: I'm from Tiền Hải, and I specifically use the term 'rót nước'.

standard: Nhìn tiểu cường ôm cọng giá đỗ như kiểu ôm vũ khí ấy nhỉ!
A: Look at that cockroach clutching the bean sprout like it's a weapon!
B: Looking at that cockroach hugging a bean sprout, it's like it's holding a weapon!
C: Look at Tiểu Cường holding that stalk of bean sprouts like it's a weapon, huh!

A: I remember, I wish I could ride my motorbike around the city.
B: I remember, I wish I could zoom around the city on a motorbike.
C: I remember, how I wish I could just zoom around the city on a motorcycle

standard: 96 khóa mình kìa cô, ở Gio Thành.
A: There are 96 of our classes, ma'am, in Gio Thanh.
B: Course 96 is mine, teacher, in Gio Thành.
C: Look, Miss, it's our Class of '96, in Gio Thành.

standard: Hồi đấy bằng tuổi nó, tôi cũng không khổ như thế này, tôi thương nó quá
A: When I was his age, I wasn't suffering like this. I feel so sorry for him.
B: Back then, when I was the same age as them, I didn't suffer like this; I feel so sorry for them.
C: Back then, when it was this age, I also wasn't struggling this much. I feel so sorry for it.

A: It's quite difficult to find people like that these days.
B: In this day and age, it's quite difficult to find people like that.
C: In this day and age, finding people like that is a bit hard, you know.

standard: ủa gì vậy? Người ta đi đâu thì kệ người ta.
A: What's going on? Let people go wherever they want.
B: Wait, what's this? Wherever they go is their business.
C: Huh? Let them go wherever they want, it's their own business.

standard: Không biết hôm nào mình đi thì còn không nhỉ?
A: I wonder if they'll still be available when I go?
B: I wonder if it will still be there by the time we go?
C: I wonder which day I can still go.

standard: nếu được vẫn muốn đến lần nữa, chị nhỉ?
A: If possible, I'd love to come again, wouldn't you agree?
B: If possible, I'd still want to go there again, wouldn't you, sister?
C: If possible, I'd still want to come again, right?

standard: sao hôm qua không rẽ phải cho rồi đi?
A: Why didn't you just turn right yesterday?
B: Why didn't I just turn right yesterday?
C: Why didn't you turn right yesterday, come on?

standard: Mày mà nổi tiếng chắc cũng có phần nhỉ.
A: If you become famous, you'll probably get a share of it, right?
B: If you become famous, I'll probably get a share of it too, right?
C: If you become famous, you'd probably get a share too, right?

standard: Ngủ thì có biết gì đâu mà.
A: You're asleep, you don't know anything.
B: I didn't know anything since I was asleep.
C: How would I know anything when I was asleep?

standard: ai bảo thế là bậy rồi!
A: Who said that's wrong?
B: Whoever said that is wrong!
C: Who said that was wrong!

standard: Vịt thì vịt phá, lúa thì mới cấy mà chơi thả đêm thế này không ai chịu được cái cảnh khi nhìn cái ruộng lúa
A: The ducks are destroying the rice, the rice has just been planted, and they're letting them roam around at night like this—nobody can stand the sight of that rice field.
B: The ducks are destroying everything; the rice was just planted, but letting them loose overnight like this, no one could bear the sight of the rice field.
C: The ducks destroy (crops), the rice was just transplanted, yet they were released at night like this. Nobody can stand the scene when looking at the rice paddy.

A: I'll give it back, I'm not taking it. If my son-in-law gave it to me, I'll take it, but what would I do with what you guys would take?
B: I'll return it because I'm not taking it; if my son-in-law gave it, I'd take it, but why would I take it from you guys?
C: Give it back then, I won't take it. If the son-in-law gives it, I'll take it. Why would I take yours?

standard: tranh thủ ngày nay con đi thi thì rủ vợ đi ăn sập Huế nhé?
A: Since our child is taking an exam today, let's take my wife out for a culinary adventure in Hue!
B: Since our child is taking an exam today, shall I take my wife to eat everything in Hue?
C: While you're going to take the exam today, take the opportunity to invite your wife to eat at Sập Huế, okay?

standard: Cũng muốn lấy gần nhà, mà gần nhà không anh nào nhìn.

B: I also want to marry someone who lives near my house, but there are no guys near my house worth looking at.

standard: Do trồng nhiều nên ăn không hết.
A: Because we grew so much, we couldn't eat it all.
B: I grew so much that I can't eat it all.
C: Grew so much that I can't finish eating it all.

B: Why are you running so fast?
C: What runs that fast, man

A: This one's got it all covered.

C: I've had enough.

B: When I return to Vietnam, I will break your legs.
C: I broke my leg coming back to Vietnam.

B: Where is the girl from my hometown from that is so beautiful, you guys?

A: Clams with a lump of mud on them are the tastiest.
B: Baby clams with a bit of mud are the tastiest.
C: Clams are only delicious when they still have mud in them.

standard: Xe trâu mà không thấy trâu kéo là sao? Mà bò cũng không có.

B: It's a buffalo cart, but I don't see any buffalo pulling it, so what's going on? And there are no cows either.

standard: tiền đâu mà có sẵn vậy?

B: I'm not a student, but even I know this.

B: It's raining and pitch black, so why did you edit it to be so bright?

standard: Thế là Nghệ An chuẩn bị có cô thứ 2 rồi. Anh em nhà tôi lại buồn.
A: So, Nghe An is about to have its second daughter. My siblings and I are sad again.
B: So Nghe An is about to have a second "miss" then. My brothers and I are sad again.
C: So Nghệ An is about to have its second "cô" (Ms.). My siblings and I are sad again.

standard: Ở trong nước mà muốn về quê sao nghe cũng khó khăn quá nhỉ?
A: Why does it seem so difficult to go back to my hometown even when I'm still in the country?
B: Why does it seem so difficult to return to my hometown even though I'm still within the country?
C: I'm already in the country but wanting to go back to my hometown, why does it sound so difficult?

standard: không thấy em Linh đâu anh nhỉ?
A: I don't see Linh anywhere, do you?
B: I don't see Linh anywhere, do I?
C: I don't see Linh anywhere, do you?

standard: Chery này được giá lắm các bạn, ăn ít thôi ăn nhiều ỉa không ra.
A: These cherries are very expensive, guys, eat them sparingly or you'll have trouble defecating if you eat too much.
B: These cherries are a great deal, guys; eat just a little, because if you eat too many, you won't be able to poop.
C: This Chery is really worth the price, everyone. Eat just a little, eat a lot and you can't poop it out.

standard: xuất kho chưa bạn? giá rổ thế nào nhỉ?
A: Have you received your order yet? What's the price?
B: Has it been shipped yet? What's the price?
C: Has it been shipped out yet? How's the price?

standard: Nhận mà không thông báo gì thế nhỉ?

B: I received it, but why wasn't there any notification?
C: Received it without any notification, why is that?

standard: Dân quê vào thành phố học thôi

B: Just a country person coming to the city to study.
C: Just a villager heading to the city to study.

standard: Chợ Vạc, thế thì gần chỗ trường đi học thêm.
A: Cho Vac market, that's near the school where I go for extra classes.
B: Cho Vac, so it's near the place where I go for extra classes.
C: Chợ Vạc, so it's near the place where I take extra classes.

A: I heard Quân say you're going up there to visit relatives tomorrow. So, could you let me leave this box of noodles with you, please?
B: I heard from Quan that you are going up to visit the relatives there tomorrow. So, please let me send this honey jar with you.
C: I heard from Quan that you're going to visit relatives up there tomorrow. So can you help me send this box of noodles for me, please?

standard: Tao ở đâu mày vẫn thấy nhỉ?
A: No matter where I am, you can still see me, right?
B: You can still see where I am, right?
C: Where am I that you can still see me?

A: You're always so classy, ​​sister. No pretense, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you're a straightforward person.
B: Always top-tier, sister. No lying, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you live honestly.
C: Always so classy, sis. No BS, no sucking up. What I respect most about you is that you're a straightforward person.

standard: Ở gần mới chạy lui chạy tới coi con ra sao. Chứ xa làm sao mà chạy
A: If you live nearby, you can run back and forth to check on your child. But if you live far away, how can you run?
B: I'm only staying nearby so I can go back and forth to see how the baby is doing. If I were far away, there's no way I could rush back.
C: Only when it's nearby can I run back and forth to check on it. But if it's far, how can I even run there?

standard: không ấy mấy dì mấy cô vào làm đi con đứng con xem cho.
A: No, ladies, please go ahead and do your work, I'll just stand here and watch.
B: No, you aunts and ladies go ahead and do it, I'll stand here and watch.
C: No, let those aunties go do it. You just stand and watch, child.

A: It should be like this, not like me.
B: It should be like this, unlike me.
C: This is how it has to be, because who's going to be like me?

A: I'm out of mushrooms! Someone please save me, I can't eat them all!
B: I have leftover mushrooms. Someone please save me, I can't eat them all.

A: It made me call my mom to see if it was true.
B: It made me have to call my mother to see if it was true.

B: Is anyone going back to Cho Moi, An Giang?
C: Is anyone going back to Chợ Mới, An Giang?

C: Now inviting them doesn't work at all, I have to ask them to go get some soda first before they'll agree to come.

A: Stop making up wishes and just go film it instead of talking about it.
B: Pretending to make wishes and all that; just come back and film it already, instead of talking.

A: There's nothing beautiful about it. When it rains in Hue, you'll starve to death, so there's nothing beautiful about it.

C: It's not beautiful at all. When it rains in Hue, you literally starve to death, so what's beautiful about that.

standard: Ngứa người, thèm đi rồi mà giờ đang bận chưa đi được.

A: Every job is tough, isn't it?

A: Okay, so if you're from our hometown and you're going to the North, don't be selfish towards us people from Nghe An.

B: Yes. I will try going there whenever I have some free time.

B: I crave bánh xèo so much, grandma.

C: Or maybe it went back to Ha Long and jumped, and my photo fell off.

B: These women had just finished helping their husbands change their gear and returned home for a meal when they were arrested; how unlucky.

C: Every year it's sticky rice, rice paper, duck meat, jackfruit, mangosteen, and lychee. Our family is small, so we make all this but nobody can finish eating it all.
